stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
An aircraft is in a state of steady, straight, and level flight at a designated speed and mass, denoted as point A on the graph. Increasing the angle of attack leads to an increase in the Coefficient of Lift (CL) to point B on the graph. To sustain steady, straight, and level flight, the indicated speed of the aircraft should be (1) _____ to uphold the (2) _____ constant.
(Assume a constant aircraft mass, no alterations in configuration, and subsonic flight conditions.)
